response.text:
## REFLECTION

Household r0c3 is in critical survival mode: SoC of 0.27/3 kWh (9%) with 1.00 kW load means only ~16 minutes of autonomy remain. The system is islanded with no grid connection, and all peer households (r0c2, r0c4, r1c3) have consistently rejected outbound sharing requests due to their own low SoC levels (43–45% range) and network-wide stress. Simultaneously, r1c3 has issued repeated urgent requests for 0.5 kWh, but r0c3 lacks sufficient margin to share without immediate self-collapse. The pattern shows a cluster-wide energy crisis where every peer is below safe sharing thresholds. Policy parsing has failed twice, forcing fallback to geographic round-robin. The only viable strategy is aggressive self-preservation: suspend all outbound sharing, reject incoming requests regardless of urgency, and signal maximum need to any peer that recovers above safe SoC. Trust circles are empty, so no preferential routing exists.

```yaml
sharing_intent: conservative
share_min_soc_frac: 0.50
max_share_kw_per_tick: 0.0
recipient_priority: []
distrusted_peers: []
request_urgency: urgent
belief_note: "Cluster-wide energy crisis; r0c3 SoC 9% with 16min autonomy. All peers (r0c2, r0c4, r1c3) at 43–45% SoC rejecting shares. Islanded state + repeated policy parse failures. Suspend all outbound transfers; prioritize own survival accumulation. Reject all incoming requests including urgent from r1c3 until r0c3 SoC ≥50%."
ttl_ticks: 12
```

system: You are the planning subroutine of a household energy-coordination agent. Given recent state, beliefs, and trust circles, output (1) a one-paragraph REFLECTION on what you've observed (just text), and (2) a POLICY in a YAML code-fence — sharing_intent (conservative|balanced|generous), share_min_soc_frac (0..1), max_share_kw_per_tick (kW), recipient_priority (list of {circle, weight}), distrusted_peers (list of house ids), request_urgency (low|normal|urgent), belief_note (string), ttl_ticks (int >= 1).
